message:
feat(bench): add RCU-vs-mutex metadata read benchmark matrix and improve elimination bench

New benchmark (bd-3aof.3):
- benches/metadata_read_bench.rs: full 7x5x3 matrix Criterion benchmark
  covering thread counts (1-64), read/write ratios (100:0 to 50:50), and
  three metadata operations (thread_metadata, size_class_lookup,
  tls_cache_lookup) comparing RCU read-side vs Mutex-based access;
  emits a complete artifact bundle under target/metadata_read_bench/
- Cargo.toml: register metadata_read_bench as a [[bench]] entry

Benchmark library (frankenlibc-bench/src/lib.rs):
- Add MetadataImplementation and MetadataOperation enums for the
  benchmark matrix dimensions
- Add MetadataBenchRecord for per-configuration measurement results
  with throughput, p50/p95/p99 latency, and coefficient of variation
- Add MetadataBreakEven type and build_break_even_summary() to compute
  the lowest read ratio where RCU throughput meets or exceeds mutex
- Add percentile_sorted(), coefficient_of_variation_pct(), and
  summarize_latency_samples() statistical helpers
- Add artifact emitters: JSON report, .dat files (throughput, latency,
  break-even), SVG visualizations, and gnuplot scripts
- Add write_metadata_bench_artifacts() to produce the full bundle
- Add unit tests for latency summarization, break-even detection, and
  artifact format validation

Elimination bench improvements:
- Replace per-thread slot selection with fixed BENCH_SLOT_BIAS constant
  for more consistent contention patterns
- Simplify mutex-queue baseline: remove Condvar-based blocking in favor
  of yield-based spinning to better match the elimination array's
  non-blocking semantics
- Remove unused Condvar import

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -60,3 +60,7 @@ harness = false
 [[bench]]
 name = "elimination_bench"
 harness = false
+
+[[bench]]
+name = "metadata_read_bench"
+harness = false
